project_empirical_status: No Planet_Earthing plant-session data have been collected yet. Current project claims are protocol, evidence-review, and analysis-design claims only.

entries.claim: Plant electrophysiological signals can be recorded from multiple species after tactile or damaging stimuli.
entries.species_or_system: >15 plant species; tactile and flame stimuli
entries.source: A library of electrophysiological responses in plants
entries.supports: Using plant electrophysiology as a measurable response channel.
entries.does_not_support: Familiar-human recognition, emotional intent detection, or sentience.
entries.design_implication: Measure electrode stability, stimulus timing, and electrical artifacts before interpreting interaction effects.

entries.claim: Brief repeated touch can change plant morphology, volatile emissions, and insect responses in a controlled plant model.
entries.species_or_system: Solanum tuberosum potato plants brushed for 1 minute per day
entries.source: Plant Responses to Brief Touching: A Mechanism for Early Neighbour Detection?
entries.supports: Touch is a biologically relevant stimulus and must be quantified.
entries.design_implication: Use pressure logging and object or robot touch controls.

entries.claim: Specific plant touch-response pathways can affect thigmomorphogenesis and growth regulation.
entries.source: Touch signaling and thigmomorphogenesis are regulated by complementary CAMTA3- and JA-dependent pathways
entries.supports: Touch responses can be molecularly regulated rather than only mechanical noise.
entries.does_not_support: That a 60 second human petting session creates a detectable person-specific signature.
entries.design_implication: Treat repeated touch as an intervention that may change plant state over time.

entries.claim: Wounding can trigger long-distance calcium-based plant defense signaling.
entries.source: Glutamate triggers long-distance, calcium-based plant defense signaling
entries.supports: Plants can produce rapid systemic signals without animal nerves.
entries.design_implication: Use wounding only as a separate positive-control domain, not as evidence for familiar-human effects.

entries.claim: Stressed plants can emit airborne ultrasonic sounds that carry information about stress condition in tested species.
entries.species_or_system: Tomato and tobacco under dehydration or cutting stress
entries.source: Sounds emitted by plants under stress are airborne and informative
entries.supports: Ultrasonic audio can be a legitimate plant-stress channel if recorded correctly.
entries.does_not_support: That gentle petting or familiar humans produce ultrasonic plant signals.
entries.design_implication: Separate plant emissions from glove, mouth, fabric, HVAC, and room sounds.

entries.claim: Electronic noses can discriminate some plant volatile signatures.
entries.source: Discrimination of Plant Volatile Signatures by an Electronic Nose
entries.supports: VOC/e-nose channels may help characterize plant state.
entries.does_not_support: That an e-nose signal during human sessions is plant-only rather than human odor, breath, humidity, or airflow.
entries.design_implication: Run blanks, airflow controls, and human-present controls; validate key claims with stronger chemistry if needed.

entries.claim: A controlled attempt to replicate Backster-style brine-shrimp primary perception did not find electrophysiological plant responsiveness.
entries.species_or_system: Plant electrophysiology during brine-shrimp killing protocol
entries.source: Plant Primary Perception: Electrophysiological Unresponsiveness to Brine Shrimp Killing
entries.supports: Treating remote intention and primary perception claims as unsupported.
entries.design_implication: Do not cite Backster as support; design direct controls instead.

entries.claim: Animal-like plant consciousness or sentience is not a working assumption for this project.
entries.source: Plants Neither Possess nor Require Consciousness
entries.supports: Keeping the project focused on measurable plant signals rather than consciousness claims.
entries.does_not_support: That plants are incapable of any measurable response or signaling.
entries.design_implication: Use neutral language: signal difference, not emotion, pain, affection, or preference.

entries.claim: Planet_Earthing has not yet produced empirical data showing host-versus-stranger differences.
entries.supports: Calling the familiar-human question untested in this project.
entries.does_not_support: Any public claim that the project has observed an effect.
entries.design_implication: README and docs must distinguish external literature from future project data.

entries.claim: Micro-Tom tomato is a compact tomato cultivar suitable for controlled model-plant research planning.
entries.source: Genetic and physiological characterization of tomato cv. Micro-Tom
entries.supports: Selecting Micro-Tom as a practical model cultivar for Phase 0 planning.
entries.does_not_support: That Micro-Tom is uniquely capable of host-versus-stranger discrimination.
entries.design_implication: Use Micro-Tom for practical pilot planning while treating biological claims as untested.
